title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Nested Object References

statement.markdown: A React application uses a memoized component to optimize re-renders of a nested object. However, the component still re-renders unnecessarily when the nested object's references change. Your task is to modify the existing implementation to prevent this behavior.
statement.inputFormat: A nested object with changing references, e.g. `{ a: { b: { c: 1 } } }`
statement.notes: The nested object's references change frequently, causing the component to re-render unnecessarily.

constraints.description: The number of nested object references

examples.explanation: The component should not re-render unnecessarily when the nested object's references change.
The component should re-render when the nested object's properties change.

hints.text: Use the `React.memo` Higher-Order Component (HOC) to optimize re-renders
Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare nested object references

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: The `React.memo` HOC can be used to optimize re-renders by comparing the props of a component. However, when dealing with nested objects, comparing references using `JSON.stringify` can lead to unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use a recursive function to compare nested object references. However, this approach can be inefficient and lead to stack overflows.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use the `React.useCallback` hook to memoize the nested object's references and compare them using `JSON.stringify`.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `React.useCallback` hook to memoize the nested object's references and compare them using `JSON.stringify`. Additionally, use the `JSON.parse` method to parse the JSON string and compare the nested object's properties.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that `JSON.stringify` returns a JSON string that represents the nested object's properties. By comparing this string, we can determine whether the nested object's references have changed.
